sideload.description: Sideloads a package to the device

install.description: Pushes a package to the device and installs it

devices.description: Prints a list of connected devices

logcat.description: Prints log data to the screen

flags.-c: Clear (flush) the entire log and exit
flags.-d: Dump the log and then exit
Direct to the only attached USB device
flags.-e: Direct to the only running emulator
flags.-s: Direct to the device with the given serial

flash.description: Write a file to a flash partition

checkout.description: Switch branches or restore working tree files

flags.-b: Create and checkout a new branch

flags.-m: Use the given message as the commit message
flags.-a: Stage all modified and deleted files
flags.--amend: Replace the tip of the current branch by creating a new commit

push.description: Update remote refs along with associated objects

pull.description: Fetch from and integrate with another repository or a local branch

flags.--rebase: Rebase local commits on top of fetched branch
flags.--no-commit: Perform the merge but do not commit

flags.-s: Give the output in the short-format

add.description: Add file contents to the index

Add all files in current directory
flags.-A: Add all files in the working tree

flags.-a: List both remote-tracking and local branches

flags.--online: Displays a list of available distributions for install
flags.--verbose: Shows detailed information about all distributions

--set-version.description: Change the version of the specified distribution

--update.description: Update the Windows Subsystem for Linux package

flags.--status: Show the status of Windows Subsystem for Linux

flags.-y: Automatically answer yes to all prompts

deploy.description: Deploy code and assets to your Firebase project

flags.--except: Deploy all features except specified, comma-separated features

init.description: Interactively configure the current directory as a Firebase workspace

serve.description: Start a local server for your static assets

flags.--port: The port on which to listen
flags.--host: The host on which to listen

flags.--reauth: Force reauthentication even if already logged in
flags.--no-localhost: Copy and paste a code instead of starting a local server for authentication

flags.-A: Print NUM lines of trailing context
flags.-B: Print NUM lines of leading context
flags.-C: Print NUM lines of output context
flags.-E: Interpret pattern as an extended regular expression
flags.-F: Interpret pattern as a list of fixed strings
flags.-G: Interpret pattern as a basic regular expression
flags.-P: Interpret pattern as a Perl regular expression
flags.-w: Force PATTERN to match only whole words
flags.-x: Force PATTERN to match only whole lines
flags.-z: A data line ends in 0 byte, not newline
flags.-s: Suppress error messages about nonexistent or unreadable files
flags.-m: Stop reading a file after NUM matching lines
flags.-b: Print the 0-based byte offset within the input file
flags.-n: Prefix each line of output with the 1-based line number
flags.-H: Print the file name for each match
flags.-h: Suppress the prefixing of file names on output
flags.-o: Print only the matched (non-empty) parts of a matching line
flags.-q: Quiet; do not write anything to standard output
flags.-a: Process a binary file as if it were text
flags.-d: If an input file is a directory, use ACTION to process it
flags.-r: Read all files under each directory, recursively
flags.-R: Read all files under each directory, recursively. Follow all symlinks
flags.-L: Suppress normal output; instead print the name of each input file from which no output would normally have been printed
flags.-l: Suppress normal output; instead print the name of each input file from which output would normally have been printed
flags.-c: Suppress normal output; instead print a count of matching lines for each input file
flags.--exclude: Skip files whose base name matches GLOB
flags.--exclude-dir: Exclude directories matching the pattern DIR from recursive searches
flags.--include: Search only files whose base name matches GLOB

flags.-p: Read input file from standard input (pipe)
flags.-w: Width of exported bitmap in pixels
flags.-h: Height of exported bitmap in pixels
flags.--export-area-drawing: Export area is the entire drawing
flags.-S: Query id, x, y, width and height

convert.description: Convert between image formats, resize, blur, crop, flip, etc. (Legacy v6 core)

identify.description: Describe the format and characteristics of one or more image files

mogrify.description: Resize, blur, crop, etc. Overwrites the original image file

montage.description: Create a composite image by combining several separate images

flags.-density: Horizontal and vertical density of the image (DPI)
flags.-alpha: Control alpha/matte channel (on, activate, off, set, copy, transparent, extract)
flags.-blur: Reduce image noise and reduce detail levels
flags.-colorspace: Alternate image colorspace (e.g., sRGB, CMYK, Gray)
flags.-auto-orient: Automatically orient image based on EXIF data
flags.-transparent: Make this color transparent within the image
flags.-trim: Trim image edges (remove solid background borders)
flags.-strip: Strip image of all profiles and comments (reduces file size)
flags.-gravity: Horizontal and vertical text/image placement (North, Center, South, etc.)
flags.-fill: Color to use when filling a graphic primitive or text
flags.-scale: Scale the image (faster than resize, no pixel blending)
flags.-resample: Change the resolution of an image
flags.-append: Append an image sequence top to bottom (-append) or left to right (+append)
flags.-fuzz: Colors within this distance are considered equal (e.g., 5%)
flags.-monochrome: Transform image to black and white
flags.-negate: Replace every pixel with its complementary color
flags.-opaque: Change this color to the fill color
flags.-profile: Add, delete, or apply an image profile
